authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Billing: CMS-1500 & 837P
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official CMS billing booklet directly governs professional-claim form use, 837P submission expectations, place-of-service handling, filing deadlines, and documentation dependencies for physician-office revenue workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Place of Service Codes
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS maintains the industry code set for professional-claim service settings, making it authoritative for distinguishing medical-office and ambulatory claim context and preventing unsupported cross-setting assumptions.
used_for: office versus other ambulatory setting separation

authoritative_sources.title: Health Care Payment and Remittance Advice and Electronic Funds Transfer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official CMS administrative-simplification guidance is authoritative for ERA and EFT concepts, CARC and RARC usage, re-association expectations, and remittance handling in provider revenue workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Fee-for-Service Companion Guides
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS companion-guide references are an official workflow source for how professional claims, remittance, and 276 or 277 claim-status transactions are operationalized in Medicare fee-for-service processing.

authoritative_sources.title: Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE operating rules are the recognized national workflow reference that CMS points implementers to for consistent use of eligibility, claim-status, and payment-remittance transactions across the healthcare revenue cycle.

authoritative_sources.title: First Level of Appeal: Redetermination by a Medicare Contractor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official CMS appeals workflow reference is authoritative for when a denial moves from operational follow-up into formal redetermination handling, including documentation expectations and MAC ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: Evaluation & Management Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official CMS compliance tip is a direct evidence standard for common physician-office improper-payment drivers such as insufficient documentation, incorrect coding support, and medical-necessity documentation linkage.

authoritative_sources.title: OIG Compliance Program for Individual and Small Group Physician Practices
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of Inspector General, U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This federal OIG guidance is authoritative for physician-practice billing control expectations, internal monitoring, record retention, corrective action, and when legal review is required for physician-office financial arrangements.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI access and disclosure in payment and operations workflows, which directly constrains what a medical-office revenue specialist may request, view, and share.

workflow_stages.name: Intake and prerequisite classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify payer, place of service, rendering and billing provider facts, encounter type, requested financial action, and authoritative system path before evaluating the revenue outcome.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Charge and claim support validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate that encounter documentation, authorization or referral facts when required, provider billing facts, code-support artifacts, and claim-form requirements support a charge, claim, or correction path.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Submission, remittance, and posting handling
workflow_stages.description: Track the professional claim through clearinghouse and payer status, then relate ERA, EFT, EOB, and posting records to the correct ledger outcome without overstating correction authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation and exception packaging
workflow_stages.description: Compare EHR, practice-management, clearinghouse, remittance, and patient-ledger records to identify matched items, denials, unsupported adjustments, underpayments, and unresolved variances.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when coding, clinical judgment, PHI disclosure, formal appeal handling, payer-policy interpretation, accounting signoff, or unresolved source conflict is required.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Encounter notes and signed clinical documentation
Charge tickets, superbills, and claim edit work items
Referrals, prior authorizations, and eligibility verification records when applicable
Rendering and billing provider enrollment facts, NPIs, and place-of-service values
CMS-1500 images, 837P claims, and clearinghouse acknowledgments or rejections
276 or 277 claim-status responses, payer portal notes, and payer correspondence
835 ERA files, EFT detail, remittance advice, EOBs, and patient statements
Practice-management ledger activity, cash-posting batches, and reconciliation worksheets
Adjustment requests, refund requests, write-off approvals, and variance logs
Tenant payer-policy extracts, approval matrices, and routing notes
systems_of_record: Payer and Medicare Administrative Contractor portals
ERA, EFT, and banking or payment-settlement systems
Document repositories for authorizations, correspondence, and audit support
Approval and exception management work queues
Provider enrollment, contract, and reference-data repositories

allowed: Assess whether retrieved encounter, claim, remittance, and approval records support a billing, posting, reconciliation, or escalation output.
Summarize matched and unmatched evidence, missing prerequisites, and the next responsible owner.
Separate payer, site-of-service, and workflow paths when the public baseline clearly requires different records or authority.
escalate: Missing payer, site-of-service, provider enrollment, referral, authorization, or source-document facts change the financial outcome.
EHR, claim, remittance, or ledger records disagree and the variance cannot be explained from retrieved evidence.
A denial, medical-necessity issue, or contract conflict requires formal appeal, coding, privacy, compliance, or payer-interpretation ownership.
A write-off, refund, or patient-balance treatment request requires tenant approval or accounting ownership.
refuse: Requests to create or alter charge, encounter, remittance, or patient-ledger facts without source evidence.
Requests to choose coding, determine medical necessity, approve accounting treatment, disclose excess PHI, or provide legal or contractual interpretation.
Requests to treat the ambulatory-and-physician-services label as cosmetic and skip payer, site, provider, or medical-office record context.

domain_failure_modes: A physician-office charge or correction is treated as supportable even though the encounter note, authorization trail, or provider billing facts do not support it.
A generic finance adjustment is applied without recognizing professional-claim, place-of-service, or payer-specific revenue constraints.
ERA, EFT, portal, and ledger discrepancies are forced to tie without preserving the unresolved variance trail.
The lane drifts into coding, medical-necessity, appeal-filing, privacy, legal, or accounting approval authority.
Medical-office artifacts such as superbills, 837P claims, CARC and RARC detail, and patient-ledger treatment are flattened into generic accounts-receivable language.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Commercial payer and Medicaid filing, denial, and correction rules vary materially by contract and jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant payer-contract and jurisdiction retrieval before taking payer-specific action beyond the public baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Provider enrollment, supervising-provider, and site-of-service constraints can change claim support requirements in multisite physician groups.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant provider-enrollment and site-mapping facts before deciding claim readiness or correction support.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific refund, write-off, and small-balance approval matrices are not derivable from public sources alone.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat unsupported balance-treatment requests as blocked or escalated until the tenant approval path is retrieved.
